LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN'S PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 8, 2014 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 21, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. THE DEFENDANT HAS WAIVED THE 5 YEARS TOLLING OF THE STATUTE. NOTICE WAS WAIVED BY BOTH SIDES. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN'S PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS DENIED. ON SEPTEMBER 8, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED PETITIONER TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A WRITTEN COPY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. THE COURT WARNED PETITIONER THAT HIS FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THAT ORDER MAY RESULT IN DENIAL OF HIS PETITION. THE PETITIONER FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. IT IS PETITIONER'S RESPONSIBILITY TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH THE RECORD. SEE FOSTER V. CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION (1983) 142 CAL.APP.3D 444, 453. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DANIEL BOREEN, TEL. NO. 564-1514, PETITIONER APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. RAFAL OFIERSKI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4244 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN's Petition For Administrative Mandamus is denied. On September 8, 2014, the court ordered petitioner to provide the court with a written copy of the administrative record. The court warned petitioner that his failure to comply with that order may result in denial of his petition. The petitioner failed to comply with the court's order. It is petitioner's responsibility to provide the court with the record. See Foster v. Civil Service Commission (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 444, 453. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Continued To Sep-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DANIEL BOREEN, TEL. NO. 564-1514, PETITIONER APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN's Petition For Administrative Mandamus is continued to September 24, 2014. Petitioner has only provided an electronic copy of the 600+ page administrative record. Petitioner shall provide a bound, paper copy of the administrative record no later than 5 court days prior to the new hearing date or the petition will be denied. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Continued To Sep-08-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DANIEL BOREEN, TEL. NO. 564-1514, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. RAFAL OFIERSKI, TEL. NO. 554--4244, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN's Petition For Administrative Mandamus is continued to September 8, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 21, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. The defendant has waived the 5 years tolling of the statute. Notice was waived by both sides. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
